### New Test Infrastructure (Sprint 2)

- **`InputTestBase`** (`Assets/_TinyRift/Tests/Helpers/InputTestBase.cs`): wraps `InputTestFixture`, auto-adds Keyboard/Mouse/Gamepad, creates asset with 2DVector composite bindings for WASD
- **`_TinyRift.Tests.asmdef`**: added `Unity.InputSystem.TestFramework` reference
- **`_TinyRift.PlayModeTests.asmdef`**: new, for future PlayMode tests
- **`PlayModeSmokeTest.cs`**: 3 basic PlayMode smoke tests (placeholders)

## Automated Tests Required

### IN-001 InputRouter Core — Logic
**Test file path**: `Assets/_TinyRift/Tests/EditMode/Input/InputRouterCoreTests.cs`
**What to test**:
- GState→Menu switches to Menu action map (AC1)
- Hold W calls `characterEntity.Move(Vector2(0,1))` each frame (AC2)
- Release all keys calls `Move(Vector2(0,0))` after previous Move (AC3)
- Mouse cursor calls `UpdateDirectionalAim(normalizedDir)` (AC4)
- InputRouter owns exactly 3 action maps: Gameplay/Menu/Camp (AC5)
- `SetActionMap` fires on every `GameStateChanged` event (AC6)
- GState→Menu while holding W flushes zero Move + zero Aim before map disable (AC7)
- GState→Loading disables all maps (AC8)
- `IInputRouter` registered as VContainer singleton in TinyRiftScope (AC9)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- GameStateChanged fires before InputRouter is ready (mitigation: subscribe in Enable())
- Null/empty map name passed to SetActionMap
- Double-Enable / double-Disable idempotency
- InputActionAsset with missing map name gracefully logs, doesn't crash

**Estimated test count**: ~12 unit tests

### IN-002 Skill Activation & Hold-to-Aim — Logic
**Test file path**: `Assets/_TinyRift/Tests/EditMode/Input/SkillActivationTests.cs`
**What to test**:
- Forward-aim press calls `UseSkill(1, Vector2.zero)` exactly once (AC1)
- Directed-aim hold 400ms then release calls `UseSkill(2, lastAimDir)` exactly once (AC2)
- `UpdateDirectionalAim()` called each frame during directed-aim hold (AC3)
- Forward-aim in slot 1 fires during directed-aim hold in slot 2 without affecting slot 2 state (AC4)
- Out-of-range skill slot (5 when only 4 exist) no-ops on press (AC5) and release (AC6)
- Two simultaneously held slots both update aim each frame and release correctly (AC7)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Zero-duration hold (press and release same frame) — forward-aim behavior?
- Device switch mid-hold (KBM → gamepad while holding)
- No formula in GDD — test cases derived from ACs + design pseudocode

**Estimated test count**: ~10 unit tests

### IN-003 Pause Toggle & Menu Navigation — Integration
**Test file path**: `Assets/_TinyRift/Tests/EditMode/Input/PauseMenuNavTests.cs`
**What to test**:
- Escape in InRun calls `TransitionTo(Paused)` (AC1)
- Escape in HeroCamp calls `TransitionTo(Paused)` (AC2)
- Escape in Paused calls `TransitionTo(InRun)` (AC3)
- Escape in Menu silently ignored (AC4)
- Escape in Victory silently ignored (AC5)
- Directed-aim cleared on pause: `UseSkill(2, lastAimDir)` flushed, held state empty (AC6)
- Menu map Enter fires focused button onClick (AC7)
- Menu map arrow keys/WASD navigate UI selectables (AC8)
- Escape in Camp → Paused → Escape returns to Camp (AC9, AC10)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- `_previousPhase` tracking: GState → Victory while paused, then unpause
- Null EventSystem at runtime
- Double-pause (Escape pressed twice before GState transitions)

**Estimated test count**: ~8 integration tests

### IN-004 Edge Cases — Logic
**Test file path**: `Assets/_TinyRift/Tests/EditMode/Input/EdgeCaseTests.cs`
**What to test**:
- Rebind W → UpArrow persists across Save/Load cycle (AC1)
- Corrupted JSON in PlayerPrefs: exception caught, default bindings active (AC2)
- Missing PlayerPrefs key: default bindings active (AC3)
- Device disconnect during directed-aim hold: skill released via `UseSkill(slot, lastDir)` (AC4)
- Device disconnect with no held skills: no exception (AC5)
- `ResetBindingsToDefaults()` clears overrides + deletes PlayerPrefs key (AC6)
- `Dispose()` unsubscribes from device change events (AC7)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Double-disconnect (already disconnected device fires another event)
- Device reconnect after disconnect (no stale state)
- PlayerPrefs key collision with other systems
- Input system test fixture availability

**Estimated test count**: ~9 unit tests

### PL-003 PoolManager Core — Logic
**Test file path**: `tests/Foundation/ObjectPooling/TestPoolManagerCore.cs`
**What to test**:
- PoolProfile pre-warm: after Start, `CountAll >= PreWarmCount` per entry (AC1)
- `PoolManager.Get(prefab)` returns valid GameObject (AC2)
- `OnSpawned()` called on returned object's IPoolable after Get (AC3)
- `PoolManager.Return(instance)` deactivates GameObject (AC4)
- `OnDespawned()` called before Return (AC5)
- Returned object reused by next Get (same instance) (AC6)
- Pooled objects parented to `_PoolRoot/{PrefabName}` in both states (AC7)
- `IPoolManager` registered as VContainer singleton (AC8)
- PoolManager wraps `ObjectPool<GameObject>` internally (AC9)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Get on empty pool (first call after pre-warm returns from pool correctly)
- Return null instance
- Return destroyed instance (MissingReferenceException)
- PoolProfile asset is null — graceful skip
- DontDestroyOnLoad pool root

**Estimated test count**: ~10 unit tests

### PL-004 Pool Safety & AOT — Logic
**Test file path**: `tests/Foundation/ObjectPooling/TestPoolManagerSafety.cs`
**What to test**:
- Pool at max: next Get grows by GrowIncrement + warning logged (AC1)
- Externally destroyed object: Return handles MissingReferenceException silently (AC2)
- ClearAll() destroys all + OnDespawned() called on each (AC3)
- Unregistered prefab Get: pool created on demand + warning logged (AC4)
- EnsureCapacity(prefab, N) grows pool to N available (AC5)
- EnsureCapacity below active count: warning logged, pool unchanged (AC6)
- ClearAll fires on scene transition (AC7)
- AOT helper compiles in IL2CPP without stripping (AC8)
- No exceptions during any operation (AC9)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- ClearAll mid-Get (single-threaded, but safety check)
- PoolProfile prefab no longer exists
- AOT type not registered in helper

**Estimated test count**: ~11 unit tests

### HD-001 Hitbox/Hurtbox Registration — Logic
**Test file path**: `tests/Foundation/HitDetection/TestHitDetectionRegistry.cs`
**What to test**:
- Register/Unregister for IHitbox and IHurtbox (AC1)
- Faction check: same-faction returns false (Player↔Player, Enemy↔Enemy) (AC2)
- Faction check: cross-faction returns true (Player↔Enemy) (AC3)
- Neutral faction collides with everyone (AC4)
- Destroyed/null hitboxes skipped without throw (AC5)
- Clear() removes all registered entries (AC6)
- IHitbox.Start() calls Register, OnDestroy() calls Unregister (AC7)
- Same for IHurtbox (AC8)
- Scene transition fires Clear() via SceneTransitionHandler (AC9)

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Late-registering spawned objects mid-combat
- DontDestroyOnLoad objects that survive scene transitions
- Duplicate Register (idempotent?)
- Register before Registry exists (must be VContainer guaranteed)

**Estimated test count**: ~10 unit tests

## Manual QA Checklist

### IN-003 Pause Toggle & Menu Navigation — Integration
**Verification method**: Manual step-through in Unity Play Mode
**Who must sign off**: Developer
**Evidence to capture**: N/A (ACs are test-verified; manual only for scene-level wiring)

Checklist:
- [ ] Press Escape in Play Mode while GState=InRun — game pauses
- [ ] Press Escape while paused — game resumes
- [ ] Verify no stale aim/move input bleeds after unpause

### PL-004 Pool Safety & AOT — Logic
**Verification method**: IL2CPP build test
**Who must sign off**: Developer
**Evidence to capture**: Build log confirming no MissingMethodException for ObjectPool<T>

Checklist:
- [ ] IL2CPP server build compiles cleanly
- [ ] PoolAotHelper.PreserveTypes() covers all ObjectPool<T> concrete types

---

## Smoke Test Scope

Critical paths to verify before QA hand-off for Sprint 2:

1. Game launches without InputSystem-related errors in console
2. WASD movement works in a test scene with InputRouter + PlayerMovement
3. Pause toggles correctly (Escape → pause → escape → resume)
4. PoolManager.Get/Return cycle works for at least one prefab type
5. AOT/IL2CPP build produces no MissingMethodException for ObjectPool types
6. HitDetectionRegistry registers/unregisters without exception

---

## Playtest Requirements

No playtest sessions required for Sprint 2. All 7 stories are Infrastructure/Foundation-layer with no player-facing changes beyond pause UI navigation. Player-facing validation deferred to Sprint 3 vertical slice integration.
